raw_consumer_statement:
We booked a hotel at XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX, TX ) through American Express Travel for XX/XX/XXXX - XX/XX/XXXX totaling {$560.00}. 

When we arrived and checked in to the hotel ( XX/XX/XXXX about XXXX ), the room was in unsatisfactory conditions. When we spoke with the front desk they stated that they didn't have any more rooms available and that they can just cancel the room since we were unsatisfied. 

We asked for a receipt for the cancellation and the guy at the front desk stated that there will be no receipt because we didn't stay in the room, and there will be no charges. I stated that I have money charged on my card and he stated since we booked through American Express that they would have to remove the charge. 

The charge was not removed from my account and disputed with American Express. American Express stated that the merchant is stating that they don't have any documentation of cancellation so they still charged me {$560.00}. 

The merchant refused to provide us with any documentation showing that we canceled the room. The cameras at the hotel clearly show us leaving the hotel around that time. We also have documentation other proof that we stayed at a different hotel that night due to there being no other rooms available. 

I need this charge removed from my card because the room was canceled and the merchant wouldn't provide cancellation paperwork. We did not stay at this hotel for the time that we were charged.
